Healing or Harming? The Silent Crisis in Healthcare Sanitation

A UNICEF Data Investigation into Sanitation in Health Facilities Worldwide

Code 
indicator2 = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_2.csv")
metadata   = pl.read_csv("unicef_metadata.csv")

print(f"Indicator 2  :  {indicator2.shape[0]} rows  x  {indicator2.shape[1]} columns")
print(f"Metadata     :  {metadata.shape[0]} rows  x  {metadata.shape[1]} columns")
print(f"Countries    :  {indicator2.select('country').n_unique()} unique")
print(f"Year range   :  {indicator2.select('time_period').min().item()} - {indicator2.select('time_period').max().item()}")
Indicator 2  :  1822 rows  x  14 columns
Metadata     :  13715 rows  x  15 columns
Countries    :  119 unique
Year range   :  2000 - 2023
Code 
print("=== obs_value summary (Indicator 2) ===")
print(indicator2.select("obs_value").describe())

print("\n=== Null counts per column ===")
nulls = indicator2.null_count()
for col in nulls.columns:
    n = nulls[col].item()
    if n > 0:
        print(f"  {col}: {n} nulls")
print("  (zero nulls in key columns: country, alpha_3_code, time_period, obs_value)")

│ null_count ┆ 0.0       │

Code 
ind2_total = (
    indicator2
    .filter(pl.col("sex") == "Total")
    .select(["country", "alpha_3_code", "time_period", "obs_value"])
)

country_avg = (
    ind2_total
    .group_by("country", "alpha_3_code")
    .agg(pl.col("obs_value").mean().alias("avg_no_sanitation"))
    .sort("avg_no_sanitation", descending=True)
)

meta_clean = (
    metadata
    .select(["country", "alpha_3_code", "year",
             "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)",
             "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)",
             "Population, total"])
    .rename({
        "Life expectancy at birth, total (years)": "life_exp",
        "GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)": "gdp_pc",
        "Population, total": "pop"
    })
    .filter(pl.col("year") >= 2015)
)

scatter_df = (
    ind2_total
    .filter(pl.col("time_period") >= 2015)
    .join(meta_clean,
          left_on=["alpha_3_code", "time_period"],
          right_on=["alpha_3_code", "year"],
          how="inner")
    .drop("country_right")
    .filter(pl.col("life_exp").is_not_null())
    .filter(pl.col("obs_value").is_not_null())
)

ts_global = (
    ind2_total
    .group_by("time_period")
    .agg(pl.col("obs_value").mean().alias("global_mean"))
    .sort("time_period")
)

country_avg_pd = country_avg.to_pandas()
scatter_pd     = scatter_df.to_pandas()
ts_pd          = ts_global.to_pandas()
top15          = country_avg_pd.head(15)

print("Data transformation complete")
print(f"  country_avg  : {country_avg_pd.shape[0]} countries")
print(f"  scatter_df   : {scatter_pd.shape[0]} rows (post-2015, joined with metadata)")
print(f"  ts_global    : {ts_pd.shape[0]} years")
print(f"  top 15 worst : {top15.iloc[0]['country']} at {round(top15.iloc[0]['avg_no_sanitation'],1)}%")
Data transformation complete
  country_avg  : 119 countries
  scatter_df   : 847 rows (post-2015, joined with metadata)
  ts_global    : 24 years
  top 15 worst : Kyrgyzstan at 63.5%
Healing or Harming? Introduction World Map Rankings Life Expectancy Trends Action

UNICEF Data 2025

UNICEF DATA REPORT  ·  GLOBAL HEALTH
Healing or Harming?
The Silent Crisis in Healthcare Sanitation Worldwide

Indicator   Proportion of health facilities with no sanitation service Coverage   119 countries Period   2000–2023 Source   UNICEF Indicator 2 + World Bank

119 countries covered

63.5% worst country (Kyrgyzstan)

2000–2023 years of data

0 meaningful improvements seen

The Crisis Nobody Talks About

When we think of hospitals and clinics, we imagine places of healing sterile, safe, life-saving. But for hundreds of millions of people across the developing world, the reality is starkly different. The very facilities meant to protect life are operating without the most basic necessity: clean sanitation.

“A healthcare facility without proper sanitation is not a place of healing it is a place of risk.” UNICEF/WHO Joint Monitoring Programme

This report examines a deeply troubling UNICEF indicator the percentage of healthcare facilities with no sanitation service. Using data from 119 countries spanning 2000 to 2023, we uncover a global crisis hiding in plain sight, one that disproportionately affects the world’s most vulnerable populations.

Why Sanitation in Healthcare Matters

Sanitation in healthcare facilities is not a luxury. It is a fundamental prerequisite for:

  • Infection prevention - absence of handwashing facilities allows pathogens to spread, particularly dangerous in post-surgical and maternity wards
  • Maternal and child safety - unsafe birth environments caused by poor sanitation contribute to neonatal infections and maternal mortality
  • Staff dignity and protocol adherence - healthcare workers cannot follow hygiene protocols without proper facilities
  • Public trust - communities that distrust facilities due to poor conditions avoid seeking care, creating a vicious cycle of worsening health outcomes

The data reveals that in some countries, over 60% of health facilities lack any sanitation service. This is not a marginal problem it is a systemic failure.

A World Divided: Global Sanitation Map

Section 02 · Visualisation 1 - World Map

The world map below visualises the average percentage of healthcare facilities with no sanitation service by country. Darker colours indicate a more severe crisis. Grey countries have no data available in the UNICEF dataset.

Code 
SHAPEFILE = "/usr/local/lib/python3.12/dist-packages/pyogrio/tests/fixtures/naturalearth_lowres/naturalearth_lowres.shp"
world_gdf    = gpd.read_file(SHAPEFILE).to_crs("EPSG:4326")
world_merged = world_gdf.merge(country_avg_pd, left_on="iso_a3", right_on="alpha_3_code", how="left")

(
    ggplot(world_merged)
    + geom_map(aes(fill="avg_no_sanitation"), color="white", size=0.2)
    + scale_fill_gradientn(
        colors=["#eaf2f8", "#aed6f1", "#2e86c1", "#1a5276", "#0d2b45"],
        na_value="#d5d8dc",
        name="% no\nsanitation",
        limits=(0, 75),
        breaks=[0, 20, 40, 60, 75],
        labels=["0%", "20%", "40%", "60%", "75%"]
    )
    + coord_cartesian(xlim=(-180, 180), ylim=(-55, 85))
    + labs(
        title    = "Healthcare facilities with no sanitation service & global overview",
        subtitle = "Average % across all available years  |  Grey = no data  |  Darker = more severe crisis",
        caption  = "Figure 1  ·  Source: UNICEF Indicator 2"
    )
    + theme_void()
    + theme(
        plot_title       = element_text(size=13, weight="bold", color="#0d2b45", margin={"b": 6}),
        plot_subtitle    = element_text(size=10, color="#555555", margin={"b": 10}),
        plot_caption     = element_text(size=8,  color="#888888"),
        legend_title     = element_text(size=9,  weight="bold", color="#1a5276"),
        legend_text      = element_text(size=8,  color="#333333"),
        legend_position  = "right",
        plot_background  = element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8", color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background = element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8", color="#eaf2f8"),
        figure_size      = (10, 5)
    )
)

Key finding: Sub-Saharan Africa and South/Central Asia bear the overwhelming burden entire regions shown in deep navy. Countries like Kyrgyzstan, Madagascar, India, Nigeria, and Ivory Coast sit at the extreme end of this spectrum. Meanwhile, Europe, North America, and parts of East Asia show near-zero rates revealing a gap that is not just geographical, but fundamentally economic and political.

The Worst Offenders: Top 15 Countries

Section 03 · Visualisation 2-Bar Chart

Which countries are most affected? The bar chart below ranks the top 15 countries by average percentage of healthcare facilities lacking sanitation. Each bar represents the mean across all years of available data, with the gradient darkening for the worst-performing countries.

Code 
top15_sorted = top15.sort_values("avg_no_sanitation", ascending=True).copy()
top15_sorted["label"] = top15_sorted["avg_no_sanitation"].round(1).astype(str) + "%"
short_names = {
    "Tanzania, United Republic of": "Tanzania",
    "Congo, the Democratic Republic of the": "DR Congo"
}
top15_sorted["country"] = top15_sorted["country"].replace(short_names)
top15_sorted["country"] = pd.Categorical(
    top15_sorted["country"],
    categories=top15_sorted["country"].tolist(),
    ordered=True
)

(
    ggplot(top15_sorted, aes(x="country", y="avg_no_sanitation", fill="avg_no_sanitation"))
    + geom_col(width=0.72)
    + geom_text(
        aes(label="label"),
        ha="left", nudge_y=0.4, size=8, color="#0d2b45", fontweight="bold"
    )
    + scale_fill_gradientn(
        colors=["#aed6f1", "#2e86c1", "#1a5276", "#0d2b45"],
        limits=(20, 70),
        guide=None
    )
    + scale_y_continuous(
        limits=(0, 82),
        breaks=[0, 20, 40, 60, 80],
        labels=["0%", "20%", "40%", "60%", "80%"]
    )
    + coord_flip()
    + labs(
        title    = "Top 15 countries: average % of healthcare facilities with no sanitation service",
        x        = "",
        y        = "Average % of facilities with no sanitation",
    )
    + theme_classic()
    + theme(
        plot_title         = element_text(size=12, weight="bold", color="#0d2b45", margin={"b": 6}),
        plot_subtitle      = element_text(size=9,  color="#555555", margin={"b": 8}),
        plot_caption       = element_text(size=8,  color="#888888"),
        axis_text_y        = element_text(size=10, color="#0d2b45"),
        axis_text_x        = element_text(size=8,  color="#555555"),
        axis_title_x       = element_text(size=9,  color="#0d2b45", margin={"t": 8}),
        panel_grid_major_x = element_line(color="#dddddd", size=0.5),
        panel_grid_major_y = element_blank(),
        plot_background    = element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8", color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background   = element_rect(fill="white"),
        figure_size        = (10, 7)
    )
)

Kyrgyzstan leads with a staggering 63.5% nearly two-thirds of its health facilities offer no sanitation service. Madagascar and Ivory Coast follow closely. What is particularly alarming is that India, the world’s most populous country, sits fifth on this list meaning the absolute number of people exposed to this risk is enormous. Each percentage point here represents not just a statistic, but real patients, real risks, and real preventable deaths.

The Life Expectancy Connection

Section 04 · Visualisation 3-Scatterplot with Regression

Is there a relationship between poor healthcare sanitation and life expectancy? The scatterplot below examines this directly, plotting the percentage of unsanitised health facilities against life expectancy at birth, with a linear regression line. Each dot represents one country (mean values, post-2015 data merged with World Bank metadata).

Code 
scatter_agg = (
    scatter_pd
    .groupby("country")
    .agg(obs_value=("obs_value", "mean"), life_exp=("life_exp", "mean"))
    .reset_index()
    .dropna(subset=["obs_value", "life_exp"])
    .query("life_exp >= 50")
)
extremes = scatter_agg.nlargest(3, "obs_value")

(
    ggplot(scatter_agg, aes(x="obs_value", y="life_exp"))
    + geom_point(aes(color="life_exp"), size=2.5, alpha=0.75)
    + geom_smooth(method="lm", color=RED, fill=RED, alpha=0.15, size=1.2)
    + geom_text(
        data=extremes,
        mapping=aes(x="obs_value", y="life_exp", label="country"),
        size=7, color="#0d2b45", nudge_y=0.8, nudge_x=-1, ha="right"
    )
    + scale_color_gradientn(
        colors=["#c0392b", "#e67e22", "#aed6f1", "#1a5276", "#0d2b45"],
        name="Life expectancy\n(years)"
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(
        name="% of health facilities with no sanitation service",
        breaks=[0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70]
    )
    + scale_y_continuous(
        name="Life expectancy at birth (years)",
        breaks=[50, 55, 60, 65, 70, 75, 80, 85],
        limits=(50, 87)
    )
    + labs(
        title    = "Poor sanitation, shorter lives: the life expectancy connection",
        subtitle = "Countries with more unsanitised health facilities tend to have lower life expectancy",
    )
    + theme_classic()
    + theme(
        plot_title       = element_text(size=12, weight="bold", color="#0d2b45", margin={"b": 6}),
        plot_subtitle    = element_text(size=9,  color="#555555", margin={"b": 8}),
        plot_caption     = element_text(size=8,  color="#888888"),
        axis_text        = element_text(size=8,  color="#555555"),
        axis_title       = element_text(size=9,  color="#0d2b45"),
        legend_title     = element_text(size=8,  weight="bold", color="#0d2b45"),
        legend_text      = element_text(size=8),
        panel_grid_major = element_line(color="#eeeeee", size=0.4),
        plot_background  = element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8", color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background = element_rect(fill="white"),
        figure_size      = (10, 6)
    )
)

The negative correlation is clear and meaningful: as the percentage of healthcare facilities without sanitation rises, life expectancy falls. Countries at the far right of the x-axis cluster in the 50-65 year life expectancy range, while countries with near-zero unsanitary facilities consistently show life expectancies above 75 years. The annotated extreme cases Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, and Ivory Coast serve as sobering reminders of how policy failure translates directly to human cost.

A Decade of Stagnation

Section 05 · Visualisation 4 - Time-Series

Has the situation improved over time? The time-series chart tracks the global average percentage of healthcare facilities with no sanitation service from 2000 to 2023. The dashed red line marks the period average of 8.0%. The amber dotted line marks the 2020 COVID-19 pandemic year.

Code 
(
    ggplot(ts_pd, aes(x="time_period", y="global_mean"))
    + geom_line(color=TEAL, size=1.4)
    + geom_point(color=NAVY, size=2.5)
    + geom_hline(
        yintercept=ts_pd["global_mean"].mean(),
        linetype="dashed", color=RED, size=0.9, alpha=0.8
    )
    + geom_vline(
        xintercept=2020,
        linetype="dotted", color=AMBER, size=1, alpha=0.9
    )
    + annotate(
        "text", x=2020.3, y=ts_pd["global_mean"].max() - 0.2,
        label="COVID-19\n2020", size=7, color=AMBER, ha="left"
    )
    + annotate(
        "text", x=2001, y=ts_pd["global_mean"].mean() + 0.25,
        label="Period average: 8.0%", size=7, color=RED, ha="left"
    )
    + scale_x_continuous(
        name="Year",
        breaks=list(range(2000, 2024, 2))
    )
    + scale_y_continuous(
        name="Global average % (no sanitation)",
        breaks=[4, 6, 8, 10, 12]
    )
    + labs(
        title    = "A decade of stagnation: global trends 2000\u20132023",
        subtitle = "Global average % of healthcare facilities with no sanitation",
    )
    + theme_classic()
    + theme(
        plot_title       = element_text(size=12, weight="bold", color=NAVY, margin={"b": 6}),
        plot_subtitle    = element_text(size=9,  color="#555555", margin={"b": 8}),
        plot_caption     = element_text(size=8,  color="#888888"),
        axis_text        = element_text(size=8,  color="#555555"),
        axis_title       = element_text(size=9,  color=NAVY),
        panel_grid_major = element_line(color="#eeeeee", size=0.4),
        plot_background  = element_rect(fill="#eaf2f8", color="#eaf2f8"),
        panel_background = element_rect(fill="white"),
        figure_size      = (10, 5)
    )
)

The time-series reveals a deeply unsettling picture. Far from showing steady improvement, the global average has oscillated without meaningful decline across more than two decades. The 2020 COVID-19 pandemic marked by the amber dotted line represents a moment when the world’s attention briefly turned to healthcare infrastructure, yet even this global shock did not produce a lasting downward shift. The trend hovering near the 8.0% period average confirms that this is not a problem in transition it is a problem in stasis.

What Needs to Happen

The data presented in this report makes one thing undeniable: healthcare sanitation is a global emergency that is not being treated as one.

Priority Actions
  1. Targeted investment in the 15 worst-affected countries especially Kyrgyzstan, Madagascar, India, and Nigeria must become a UNICEF funding priority
  2. Routine data collection must be strengthened in countries with data gaps (shown in grey on the world map) to ensure accountability
  3. Life expectancy targets cannot be met without addressing healthcare sanitation this is a core determinant of health, not a peripheral issue
Key Numbers from the Data
63.5%Kyrgyzstan-worst globally
119countries with data
23 yrszero meaningful decline
~50 yrsavg life exp. worst cases

The world cannot call itself committed to universal healthcare while millions of patients enter facilities that lack a working toilet. The data demands action. The question is whether the world has the will to deliver it.

About This Report

Detail Value
Indicator Proportion of health care facilities with no sanitation service
Supporting data World Bank development metadata life expectancy, GDP per capita, population
Countries covered 119 countries across all inhabited continents
Time period 2000 – 2023 (24 years)
Visualisation tools plotnine (all 4 charts) · GeoPandas (world map) · Polars (data transformation)
